programmes.show_description: H from Steps has your ultimate party playlist. Not just for Fridays!

Everything you need to know as you wake up.
Your dedications and song requests from the 1950s-90s. Can you guess the Stuck in the Middle song? Text 81012, email moneyfornothing@bbc.co.uk or call 03700 100 110.
Lucy Owen brings great music and guests to your Saturday.
Live commentary, guests and analysis. Your home for Welsh sport.
Bethan chats to Dafydd Owain and shares his live music from Neuadd Ogwen in Bethesda. Plus brilliant new Welsh music in The Wales Mix.
Adam catches up with Ailsa Tully, we dive into the vault to uncover an interview with Andrew Falkous, and there's the best in new Welsh music.
Relax, unwind and chill out with an hour of classic and contemporary tunes.

Gary Raymond and guests bring you the Welsh arts scene.

programmes.show_name: We Can't Go On Eating Like This!
programmes.show_description: The way our children eat will go a long way to determining their future. How concerned should we about their diets and the lure of fast food? What can be done to reverse the trend?
From Ceredigion to Nefyn, Ifan Jones Evans and Rhodri Davies explore the role of village pubs at the heart of rural community life.
The Reverend Paul Robinson leads a service looking at Jesus and Lazarus, and reflecting on what happens when Jesus doesn't do what we think he should.
Nick Servini with political news, discussions and analysis.
Rosa Hunt talks to Phillip Henry, a Rastafarian beekeeper in Cardiff, about religion and biodiversity.
A second dose of Owen Money for the weekend. It's nonstop music all the way, with classics from the 60s, 70s and 80s, plus your dedications!
Robin brings you the best in music and guests for your Sunday afternoon. Call 03700 100 110 Text 81012 or message us via X @bbcradiowales.
Music, entertainment and great guests for your Sunday afternoon.
Roy chats with Welsh harpist Catrin Finch about her career and new collection of music. Also, we hear about celebrations for the 200th birthday of the town of Maesteg.
What happens after the news crews leave? Catrin Nye presents a series about the aftermath and long-term effects of tragedy and trauma on humans and a community.
The Reverend Paul Robinson leads a service looking at Jesus and Lazarus, and reflecting on what happens when Jesus doesn't do what we think he should.
Frank Hennessy presents the best in folk, roots and acoustic music from the Celtic countries and beyond.
The finest musical melodies, exquisite performances from stage and screen, and orchestral and choral works from Wales and the world. There's no need to book your seat, just listen and relax.
